title: Brevo vs Klaviyo (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Brevo and Klaviyo email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which platform suits your business best.
h1: Brevo vs Klaviyo: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: Both Brevo and Klaviyo are leading email marketing platforms founded in 2012, but they serve different business needs. Brevo excels as an all-in-one CRM with multi-channel capabilities, while Klaviyo specializes in e-commerce automation. This comparison will help you choose the right platform based on your marketing goals and budget.
choose_a_if: You need an affordable all-in-one CRM with email, SMS, WhatsApp, and live chat capabilities
You want to use the platform with a large number of free contacts (up to 100,000)
You prioritize transactional email capabilities and multi-channel marketing integration
choose_b_if: You run an e-commerce store on Shopify or WooCommerce and need specialized integrations
You want advanced predictive analytics and intelligent segmentation for customer behavior
You need platform-specific features like product reviews and e-commerce-focused automation flows

faq.a: It depends on your needs. Brevo is better for businesses wanting an affordable, multi-channel CRM with generous free tiers and transactional email capabilities. Klaviyo excels for e-commerce brands seeking specialized Shopify/WooCommerce integrations and predictive analytics. Both have strong ratings, with Klaviyo slightly higher on G2 (4.6 vs 4.5) but Brevo stronger on Trustpilot (4.3 vs 3.8).

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Brevo or Klaviyo?
faq.a: Brevo is significantly cheaper, starting at $25/month compared to Klaviyo's $45/month. More importantly, Brevo's free tier allows up to 100,000 contacts, while Klaviyo limits free users to just 500 contacts. This makes Brevo the clear winner for budget-conscious businesses and startups.

faq.q: Can I switch from Klaviyo to Brevo?
faq.a: Yes, you can migrate from Klaviyo to Brevo. Both platforms support contact list imports and standard email formats. However, you may lose some Klaviyo-specific features like predictive analytics during the switch. We recommend planning your migration carefully and testing workflows before fully transitioning.
